repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:
No way to handle a "tab moved" event.
Use-case: tabline plugins want to cache tab labels, and need to know when to invalidate their cache.

Solution:
Add a `TabMoved` event that triggers whenever tabs are reordered via `:tabmove` or via mouse click-and-drag.

See #6916 for a more detailed discussion of use cases, etc.

fix #23911
fix #6916

comment_data.body:
these should be |event-data|, we are deprecating `v:event`. and it looks like your impl already does that, just need to fix this doc.

use this template: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/b62c1049c09997c956f91e463361e3c21e3f52c6/runtime/doc/autocmd.txt#L845-L848
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1165,6 +1165,15 @@ TabEnter			Just after entering a |tabpage|.
 TabLeave			Just before leaving a |tabpage|.
 				After WinLeave.
 
+							*TabMoved*
+TabMoved			After a |tabpage| is moved.
+				See |:tabmove|. <amatch> expands to the
+				affected |tabpage-number| before the move.
+				Sets these |v:event| keys:

comment_data.body:
this should be on `event-args` rather than `event-data`. the practical difference for the user is `ev.tab` instead of `ev.data.tab`, but it's also for consistency.

- https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/25844#issuecomment-1843845378

this could be a followup if it's too involved for this PR. i'm not sure what code changes will be required. but for now at least, drop `tabid` from this PR.
this could be in the above test, no need for separate `it()` blocks. that increases coverage too, because each assert can test more state.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+local t = require('test.testutil')
+local n = require('test.functional.testnvim')()
+
+local api = n.api
+local clear = n.clear
+local command = n.command
+local poke_eventloop = n.poke_eventloop
+local eval = n.eval
+
+local eq = t.eq
+
+describe('TabMoved', function()
+  before_each(function()
+    clear()
+  end)
+
+  it('sets <amatch> to the the old tab page number', function()
+    -- start on tabpage 1, create 2 more so tabpage 3 is "current tab"
+    command('tabnew')
+    command('tabnew')
+    command([[
+      let g:amatch_log = []
+      au! TabMoved * call add(g:amatch_log, expand('<amatch>'))
+    ]])
+
+    command('tabmove 0')
+    poke_eventloop()
+    eq({ '3' }, eval('g:amatch_log'))
+
+    command('tabnext 2')
+    command('tabmove $')
+    poke_eventloop()
+    eq({ '3', '2' }, eval('g:amatch_log'))
+
+    command('tabmove -1')
+    poke_eventloop()
+    eq({ '3', '2', '3' }, eval('g:amatch_log'))
+  end)
+
+  it('sets all fields in v:event', function()

comment_data.body: Done. Converted the test to Lua as well to avoid new uses of `v:events`.

comment_data.body:
This will require at least additions to `aucmd_defer`, and `AutoCmdEvent` all the way through this `au_callback` function. I am not familiar w/ this codebase, so likely more places too. This is more involved than I want to deal with. 

I've dropped `tabid`. API consumers can re-derive it with `nvim_list_tabpages()`.
Done. I converted the test to Lua as well to avoid new uses of `v:event`.

comment_data.body: do we need a mouse-move test? example: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/5ba0d28634d031750fad97fd41dcf7a63d63d564/test/functional/ui/cmdline_spec.lua#L1698
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+local t = require('test.testutil')
+local n = require('test.functional.testnvim')()
+
+local clear = n.clear
+local command = n.command
+local exec_lua = n.exec_lua
+local poke_eventloop = n.poke_eventloop
+local eval = n.eval
+
+local eq = t.eq
+
+describe('TabMoved', function()
+  before_each(function()
+    clear()
+  end)
+
+  it('sets <amatch> and event-data fields', function()
+    -- start on tabpage 1, create 2 more so tabpage 3 is "current tab"
+    command('tabnew')
+    command('tabnew')
+
+    exec_lua([[
+      _G.tm_events = {}
+      vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('TabMoved', {
+        callback = function(ev)
+          table.insert(_G.tm_events, { match = ev.match, data = ev.data })
+        end,
+      })
+    ]])
+
+    command('tabmove 0')
+    poke_eventloop()
+    local events = exec_lua('return _G.tm_events')
+    eq('3', events[1].match)
+    eq(3, events[1].data.old_tabnr)
+    eq(1, events[1].data.new_tabnr)
+
+    command('tabnext 2')
+    command('tabmove $')

comment_data.body: Fixed. Please nitpick away -- glad to have your design guidance :)

Done. I may be restarting nvim needlessly in this test, glad for feedback. 

When I queued the click and the drag without a restart, I received autocmd events for a "ghost drag". I believe this was due to  because I didn't properly clear the UI state in between assertions in my test (since even unrelated UI code was triggered twice). Couldn't figure out how to clear the state, so I followed `cmdline_spec.lua`'s example and just used `testnvim.clear`.

comment_data.body: maybe it's more useful for this to be the tab-id ?

comment_data.body:
It would be more useful, but would also be inconsistent with tabclose and the others. 

I figured if a new args.tab is in the works, then it would resolve all of these autocmds at once. Until then I opted to copy tabclose and avoid breaking the principle of least surprise. 

Happy to implement whichever you prefer.

comment_regular_data.body:
This would really help for https://github.com/MunifTanjim/nougat.nvim.

Currently there is no way to invalidate the cache when a tab has moved.

This https://github.com/MunifTanjim/nougat.nvim/blob/a9fdb6107a92e36a4c76b50c8967001097deb29e/lua/nougat/nut/tab/tablist/label.lua#L10:

```lua
local function get_content(_, ctx)
  local tab_ctx = ctx.tab
  o_label_opts.tabnr = tab_ctx.tabnr
  return core.label(vim.fn.fnamemodify(vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(tab_ctx.bufnr), ":t"), o_label_opts)
end
```

generates the tab label `%2Tfilename%T` which is cached. When the 2nd tab is moved to 3rd position, the cache is not invalidated so it remains `%2T...` and clicking it does unexpected things. `TabMoved` event would have helped invalidating the cache.

cc: @zeertzjq

@MunifTanjim: does the API I've proposed in this PR work for you? Do you need additional information about the move via `<amatch>` or `<afile>`? If it works for you, I want to quickly fix my PR and push it.

> @MunifTanjim: does the API I've proposed in this PR work for you? Do you need additional information about the move via <amatch> or <afile>? If it works for you, I want to quickly fix my PR and push it.

If I get access to `tabid` and `tabnr`, it would be very useful. But that goes for all other autocmds related to tabs too (e.g. `TabClosed`, `TabNew`, `TabEnter` etc.).

I'll set `<afile>` to the pre-move tab page number, which is consistent with `TabClosed`'s behavior. 

Hopefully that's enough for you to do what you need. If not, please feel free to suggest a better API: eg: 

- Setting `<afile>` to the _post_-move tab page number
- Setting `<afile>` to the pre-move tab page number, and `<amatch>` to the post-move tab page number. 

(I'm not educated enough in plugin development to understand which is a better API). 

Also --  I'm not sure what you mean by `tabid`. I couldn't find it in the documentation either.

> Also -- I'm not sure what you mean by tabid. I couldn't find it in the documentation either.

Yeah, it's not documented clearly. It's mentioned as `tabpage` in `:help api-tabpage`. It's a unique identifier of the tab. Moving a tab left/right will change the tab page number, but the unique identifier (tab page id) remains the same.

Ah, thanks! Yes, that sounds very useful. I'll add this to the API as either amatch or afile.

I don't think I'll be able to spend much time in this for the next few months due to a work deadline. But with luck, you will no longer be using your hack by October:)

Calling `apply_autocmds` in random, new places is always a risk. 

To unblock this, I think `TabMoved` may be another candidate that can be implemented with this approach:

- https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/35366

Given a "queued-autocommand" API, I'm happy to adapt this patch to that API and wrap this up. But I don't have the availability to design an API.

I'm following Issue #35366; when that implementation finalizes, I'll follow their lead here (unless someone else wants to take charge on this).

Great, PR #35793 has unblocked this PR by creating an `aucmd_defer()` API. Just need to switch this patch to (a) call `aucmd_defer()` (b) pass the `TabID` as an autocmd argument. 

I will try to complete this before the holidays, and wrap up this long-unfinished business...

Ready for review. 

I'm not very familiar w/ autocmd conventions, so would be glad for your feedback on the API. As of now, `<amatch>` is the tabnr of the moved tab (pre-move), `<afile>` is empty, and the destination tabnnr and tabid are in `v:events`. This follows the conventions set by `TabClosed` and the `MarkSet`. One concern: I didn't set `<afile>` to the same value as `<amatch>`. `TabClosed` sets both to the same val...but it seems like an exception among other autocmds, so I opted to mimic the newer autocmd APIs instead.
doesn't look like any changes were pushed

title: feat(events): add TabMoved autocommand event
